title: Alnylam — Contacts
html:
Module 4: Alnylam Contact Map & Relationship Intelligence

Governing Thought

Alnylam's client-side organization is a compact rare disease insights team -- significantly smaller than J&J's 30+ person stakeholder network, but with a higher ratio of decision-making authority per contact. The weekly check-in meetings (22+ documented through April 2026) indicate a deeply embedded partnership where ZoomRx has regular access to the client team. The contact map below captures what is currently known from ZoomRx study documentation and interactions; significant gaps remain in executive-level and field medical contacts that should be populated through the next client interactions.

Alnylam Insights Organization Structure

[Populate from next client interaction and AO plan. Structure below is inferred from study documentation.]

[VP/Sr. Director, Market Insights -- TBD]
  |
  |--- [Director, TTR Market Insights -- TBD]
  |      |--- [Assoc. Director / Manager, AMVUTTRA -- TBD]
  |
  |--- [Director, Rare Disease Market Insights -- TBD]
  |      |--- Elizabeth Bergantino -- Market Insights (GIVLAARI / OXLUMO)
  |
  |--- [Field Medical / MSL Leadership -- TBD]

Tier 1: Day-to-Day Contacts (Known)

These are the contacts confirmed through ZoomRx study documentation and client interactions.

Rare Disease (GIVLAARI / OXLUMO)

Name Title Email Context
Elizabeth Bergantino Market Insights [Populate from Kimble / AO plan] Primary client contact for GIVLAARI and OXLUMO studies. Named in the GIVLAARI knowledge doc as the Alnylam-side point of contact. Likely involved in both PET and ATU study design, fielding decisions, and readout scheduling.

ZoomRx Team (Internal)

Name Role Email Scope
Sneha Shah Sr. Manager sneha.shah@zoomrx.com Primary ZoomRx contact for Alnylam rare disease (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO studies). Named in both PH1 and AHP documentation
Richard Durante Principal [Populate] Principal on the account
Pranav UT Sr. Consultant [Populate] GIVLAARI study team
Gauri Srivastava Consultant [Populate] GIVLAARI study team
Sugandha Anand Sr. Consultant [Populate] GIVLAARI study team

Tier 2: Decision Makers & Influencers (To Be Mapped)

The contacts below are roles that must exist given the scope of ZoomRx's engagement but have not yet been confirmed by name from available data. Populate these through the next 2-3 client interactions.

TTR Franchise (AMVUTTRA / ONPATTRO)

Role Expected Scope Populate From
VP/Sr. Director, US Market Insights Budget authority across all TAs; approves study scope and renewals AO plan, next executive interaction
Director, TTR Market Insights Owns AMVUTTRA US PET, ATU, PCA, Integrated Analysis program Weekly check-in meetings
AMVUTTRA US Brand Lead (Insights) Day-to-day contact for US PET and ATU; quarterly wave approvals Weekly check-in meetings
International PET Lead Manages 5-country international PET program (France, Italy, Germany, Spain, Japan) International PET readouts
ATTR-CM Commercial Lead Post-ATTR-CM approval (March 2025), likely a dedicated commercial insights person for cardiology segment Strategy study contacts
ATTR Strategy Lead Owns the AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy study and strategic imperatives mapping Q1 2025 Integration deck

Rare Disease

Role Expected Scope Populate From
Head of Rare Disease Insights Oversees GIVLAARI + OXLUMO insights portfolio AO plan, escalation meetings
Global ATU Lead (GIVLAARI) Manages the US/Germany/Brazil ATU program Global ATU Wave 4 interactions
PH1 Brand Insights Lead May overlap with Elizabeth Bergantino or be separate given OXLUMO PET expansion OXLUMO PET readouts

Field Medical / MSL

Role Expected Scope Populate From
Head of TTR Field Medical Manages ATTR MSL team; relevant if MEE or MSL effectiveness work expands Client interactions
Rare Disease MSL Leadership GIVLAARI and OXLUMO MSLs PET MSL impact data shows MSL interactions tracked

Cardiology Commercial

Role Expected Scope Populate From
Head of US Cardiology Sales Post-ATTR-CM launch, Alnylam expanded cardiology field force significantly Cardiologist reach data in PET
Cardiology Field Force Training Responsible for cardiologist message optimization -- directly relevant to PET message effectiveness data PET readouts showing cardiologist-specific gaps

Tier 3: Adjacent Stakeholders (To Be Identified)

Stakeholder Type Why They Matter How to Identify
International Commercial Leads (by country) ZoomRx runs 5-country international PET; each country has a local commercial team receiving readouts International PET deliverable distribution lists
Market Access / Payer Strategy Access/affordability is the #1 barrier across all three TAs; the payer strategy team is a natural audience for ZoomRx insights ATU readouts on access barriers
Medical Affairs / RWE Real-world evidence needs are growing (combination therapy in ATTR, chronic symptom outcomes in AHP, transplant deferral in PH1) Client requests for RWE integration
Competitive Intelligence Alnylam monitors Pfizer, BridgeBio, AstraZeneca/Ionis, and Novo Nordisk actively Ferma Congress or CI-adjacent projects
R&D / Pipeline ALN-TTRsc04 (next-gen TTR silencer) is in Phase 1; pipeline-to-commercial transition creates new study opportunities Pipeline readout timing

Relationship Intelligence

What We Know About Working with Alnylam

Meeting Cadence:

  • Weekly check-in meetings: 22+ meetings documented through April 2026 (most recent: 04.29.2026). This is an unusually high meeting cadence for a non-J&J account, signaling deep partnership
  • Study-specific cadences: PET is quarterly (ATTR US), semi-annual (GIVLAARI), and wave-based (OXLUMO, International PET); ATU is annual; PCA is semi-annual

Communication Style (Inferred from Study Documentation):

  • Alnylam is detail-oriented on methodology: target list segmentation (Ultra/High/Medium/Low/Exploratory), survey length management (26-minute ATU approaching limits), and recruitment match rates are actively discussed
  • Cross-study integration is valued: the Integrated Analysis (PET+ATU+PCA) and the HCP Adoption (ADOPT) funnel framework demonstrate appetite for connected insights, not siloed deliverables
  • Real-time competitive intelligence matters: Wainua fielding was paused strategically pending CARDIO-TTRansform data; ATTRUBY tracking was added rapidly after approval -- the client expects ZoomRx to be responsive to market events

Study Design Involvement:

  • Alnylam provides detailed target lists with HCP segmentation tiers that directly affect recruitment
  • New target list (April 2026) reduced available respondents by 56-72% -- significant methodology impact requiring collaborative problem-solving
  • Survey length pressure is a recurring theme: ATU at 26 minutes approaching 35-minute upper limit; tradeoffs between standardizing methodology (MaxDiff) and accommodating four product loops are actively debated

Relationship Building Priorities

Priority Action Rationale
HIGH: Map the TTR insights leadership Identify and build relationships with the AMVUTTRA-specific contacts through weekly check-in meetings TTR is 80%+ of Alnylam revenue and the most complex research program; ZoomRx needs named contacts at every level
HIGH: Understand the post-ATTR-CM org changes Ask about cardiology-specific hires and team restructuring during next interaction ATTR-CM approval (March 2025) likely triggered organizational expansion; ZoomRx needs to know who owns the cardiology insights agenda
MEDIUM: Build international PET relationships Identify country-level contacts through international PET readout attendance 5-country PET is a significant engagement; country-level contacts may have different priorities than the US team
MEDIUM: Connect with Market Access Proactively share access/affordability insights from ATU and PET with the payer strategy team Access barriers are the #1 finding across AHP and PH1; making ZoomRx's data visible to the access team creates a new internal champion
LOW: Map R&D / Pipeline contacts Watch for ALN-TTRsc04 data readouts as a trigger for new relationship introductions Pipeline transitions create study opportunities; early relationships prevent competitor vendors from filling the gap

Contact Map Gaps (Action Items)

The following information gaps should be closed through the next 2-3 client interactions:

  • Full org chart for Alnylam US Market Insights team (names, titles, reporting lines)
  • Named contacts for AMVUTTRA/TTR franchise insights (currently zero confirmed names)
  • AO/AD assignment -- who at ZoomRx is the Account Owner and Account Director for Alnylam?
  • Cluster assignment -- which cluster (East 6, 7, or 8) owns Alnylam projects?
  • Email addresses for all known contacts
  • International PET stakeholder list (country-level contacts in France, Italy, Germany, Spain, Japan)
  • Working style notes for Elizabeth Bergantino and key unnamed contacts
  • Budget holder identification -- who controls Alnylam's market research budget?
  • Preferred vendor status -- does ZoomRx have preferred pricing with Alnylam? What is the discount structure?

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
